title: salix × propinqua
intro: Salix × propinqua, also known as the cleft sallow or weeping willow, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between the white willow (Salix alba) and the weeping willow (Salix babylonica). The medicinal properties of Salix × propinqua lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has been used to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The health benefits of Salix × propinqua include its ability to help manage fever, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its potential to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Salix × propinqua can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its effects.
